title: Determining structures of RNA conformers using AFM and deep neural networks
methods: unsupervised machine learning with PCA and clustering (UML)
supervised deep neural network for r.m.s.d. estimation
FARFAR2 (Rosetta) RNA structure prediction for initial models
Fourier ring low-pass filtering for image processing
data_sources: NCI institutional data site (HorNet benchmark data, PDBs, AFM images)
FARFAR2 Rosetta structure prediction ensemble (10,000 models)
Cypher VRS AFM (Asylum Research) experimental images

data_scope.psdatabase_models: 3.5 million continuous trajectory structures (RPR catalytic domain)

core_conclusion: HORNET (Holistic RNA structure determination using AFM, unsupervised machine learning, and deep neural networks) determines 3D topological structures of individual heterogeneous RNA conformers from single-molecule AFM images without signal averaging. Applied to six benchmark RNAs (approximately 56 million trajectory models), three RNase P RNA conformers, and five HIV-1 RRE conformers, it achieves top-10-model accuracies below approximately 6 Angstroms r.m.s.d., addressing a major gap in structural biology left by NMR, crystallography, and cryo-EM for flexible and conformationally heterogeneous RNAs.
why_it_matters: First method to determine 3D RNA structure from a single molecule without signal averaging; fills the structural-biology gap left by AlphaFold for RNA; resolves long-standing HIV-1 RRE structural debate enabling rational drug design; DNN generalizes across RNA families without retraining; provides built-in quantitative accuracy estimation (analogous to AlphaFold pLDDT) for unknown conformers.
why_nature: Breakthrough methodological paradigm (single-molecule structure determination); cross-disciplinary integration of AFM, molecular dynamics, and deep learning; broad biomedical relevance (RNA therapeutics, viral replication); open data and code deposited at NCI and Zenodo; mechanism-to-application completeness (structural discovery leading directly to novel branched-peptide therapeutic design in the same paper).
added_value_to_my_research: Topographic constraint as a physics-informed prior reducing data requirements — analogue for geospatial foundation model fine-tuning with scarce labels
Probabilistic conformational ensemble estimation from single-molecule data — template for uncertainty-aware spatial prediction in GWR/MGWR models
GNN surrogate force field replacing expensive MD — analogue for GNN-ODE replacing agent-based epidemic/urban simulations
Multi-modal restraint integration (AFM + SAXS + ion mobility) — blueprint for orthogonal multi-source fusion (remote sensing + mobile phone + social media) to reduce spatial uncertainty
Population-scale conformational atlas via standardized pipeline — open science and FAIR data model directly informing reproducible multi-source geospatial data workflows

related.title: Learning on topological surface and geometric structure for 3D molecular generation
related.relation: generative model conditioned on molecular topography as direct extension of HORNET's discriminative DNN

related.title: Learning conformational flexibility of immune receptors
related.relation: probabilistic ensemble prediction of conformational states as extension of HORNET's point r.m.s.d. estimation

related.title: Rapid protein model refinement by deep learning
related.relation: GNN-based surrogate for protein structure refinement as template for replacing HORNET's Langevin MD

related.title: Protein shape sampled by ion mobility mass spectrometry consistently improves protein structure prediction
related.relation: multi-modal shape restraint integration as extension of HORNET's AFM-only restraint

related.title: Clustering single-cell RNA-seq data with a model-based deep learning approach
related.relation: model-based deep learning clustering at population scale as template for RNA conformational atlas construction

related.title: Geometric deep learning of particle motion by MAGIK
related.relation: geometric deep learning for single-particle trajectory analysis as methodological complement to HORNET's AFM image-to-structure pipeline

figures.caption: Overall HORNET pipeline — from AFM topography input through dynamic fitting, UML/DNN clustering, to 3D structure output; psDatabase composition (3.5M models) and benchmark partitioning (~56M total)
Raw and processed AFM particle images of RPR (P1-P3) with resolution estimation (ACV), cross-correlation to crystal structure, and noise characterization at 7 Gaussian noise levels (5-50%)

UML pipeline benchmarks (BM0-BM5): PCA cluster plots, energy contours, violin plots of energies and CC_AFM, r.m.s.d. progression through selection stages, and lowest-energy structures colored by r.m.s.f.

DNN performance: true vs. estimated r.m.s.d. scatter plots for BM1-BM5 (Pearson r = 0.77-0.95), loss curves vs. epoch, UML vs. DNN comparison violin plots, and r.m.s.e. uncertainty across ~5.4 million independent structures

Three experimental RPR conformers (P1-P3): energy-CC_AFM contour maps, top recapitulated molecular surface models (estimated accuracy 4-6 Angstroms), DNN accuracy distributions, and r.m.s.f. profiles per residue
HIV-1 RRE five conformers (C0-C4): AFM images and HORNET-recapitulated 3D structures, Rev-binding site distances (45-70 Angstroms range), r.m.s.f. per residue, branched peptide design, and EMSA gel
